title: Arrays & Hashing
description: Core array manipulation techniques and hash map patterns enabling O(1) average-case lookup. The foundation of most interview problems.

title: Two Pointers
description: Technique of maintaining two indices that traverse a structure simultaneously, reducing O(n²) brute force to O(n).

title: Sliding Window
description: Maintains a variable or fixed-size window over a sequence to find subarray/substring conditions in O(n) time.

title: Binary Search
description: Divide-and-conquer search in O(log n) on sorted arrays. Template extends to search on answer space.

title: Linked Lists
description: Node-based linear structures. Key patterns: fast/slow pointers, reversal in-place, cycle detection.

title: Trees & BST
description: Recursive and iterative tree traversal (pre/in/post/level-order). BST invariants for O(log n) search, insert, delete.

title: Heaps / Priority Queue
description: Min/max heap for O(log n) insert and O(1) peek. Applications: k-th element, merge k sorted lists, task scheduling.

title: Graphs (BFS/DFS)
description: Graph traversal with BFS (shortest path, level-by-level) and DFS (connected components, cycle detection, topological order).

title: Dynamic Programming
description: Optimal substructure + overlapping subproblems. Top-down memoization vs bottom-up tabulation. State transition design is the core skill.

title: Greedy Algorithms
description: Making locally optimal choices that yield global optimum. Requires proving the greedy property holds — interval scheduling, activity selection.

title: Backtracking
description: Systematic exploration of all candidates with early pruning. Core pattern for permutations, combinations, constraint satisfaction.

title: Tries
description: Prefix tree for O(m) string search where m is word length. Essential for autocomplete, IP routing, word search in grids.

title: Advanced Graph Algorithms
description: Dijkstra (SSSP with non-negative weights), Bellman-Ford (negative edges), Floyd-Warshall (APSP), Topological Sort (DAG ordering).

title: Segment Trees
description: Range query + point update in O(log n). Lazy propagation for range updates. Used in competitive programming and some systems interviews.

title: Union-Find (Disjoint Set Union)
description: Near O(1) amortized union/find with path compression + union by rank. Used for connectivity, Kruskal's MST, cycle detection in undirected graphs.

title: Linear Algebra for ML
description: Vectors, matrices, dot products, eigendecomposition, SVD, matrix calculus. The language that all ML is written in.

title: Probability & Statistics
description: Probability rules, distributions (Gaussian, Bernoulli, Poisson), MLE, MAP, Bayes theorem, hypothesis testing, CLT.

title: Optimization (GD/SGD/Adam)
description: Gradient descent variants (batch/mini-batch/SGD), adaptive methods (Adam, RMSprop), learning rate schedules, convergence theory.

title: Bias-Variance Tradeoff
description: Decomposition of generalization error into bias² + variance + irreducible noise. Underfitting vs overfitting. Model complexity tradeoffs.

title: Linear & Logistic Regression
description: OLS derivation, normal equations, gradient descent training. Logistic regression as probabilistic binary classifier, cross-entropy loss.

title: Decision Trees & Random Forests
description: Information gain / Gini impurity splits, CART algorithm, pruning. Random forests as bagged ensembles with feature subsampling.

title: Support Vector Machines
description: Maximum margin classifier, kernel trick (RBF, polynomial), soft-margin SVM, dual formulation, SMO algorithm.

title: XGBoost / Gradient Boosting
description: Boosting as additive model training with gradient descent in function space. XGBoost's regularization, tree structure scoring, and SHAP values.

title: Regularization (L1/L2/Dropout)
description: L1 (sparse weights via soft-thresholding), L2 (weight decay), Dropout (ensemble approximation), early stopping. Bayesian interpretation.

title: Feature Engineering
description: Encoding (one-hot, target, embeddings), scaling, binning, interaction features, handling missing data, feature selection methods.

title: Evaluation Metrics & Calibration
description: Precision/recall/F1, ROC-AUC, PR-AUC, calibration (Platt scaling, isotonic regression), confusion matrices, class imbalance handling.

title: Unsupervised Learning (K-Means/GMM/PCA)
description: K-Means (Lloyd's algorithm, k-means++), GMM with EM algorithm, PCA via SVD, t-SNE, UMAP for visualization.

title: Bayesian Methods
description: Bayesian inference, conjugate priors, MCMC (Gibbs sampling, HMC), variational inference, Gaussian processes, Bayesian optimization.

title: Causal Inference
description: Potential outcomes framework, ATE/ATT, instrumental variables, difference-in-differences, regression discontinuity, do-calculus.

title: ML Theory (PAC Learning / VC Dimension)
description: PAC learnability, sample complexity bounds, VC dimension, Rademacher complexity, generalization bounds, no-free-lunch theorem.

title: Backpropagation from Scratch
description: Forward pass, computational graphs, chain rule for gradient accumulation at split nodes, autograd implementation. The most important concept in deep learning.

title: CNNs & Pooling
description: Convolution as feature detection, receptive fields, max/avg pooling, stride, padding. BatchNorm, residual connections (ResNet).

title: RNNs & LSTMs
description: Recurrent computation, vanishing gradient problem, LSTM cell (input/forget/output gates), GRU, sequence-to-sequence models.

title: Attention Mechanism
description: Scaled dot-product attention, query/key/value framework, attention weights as soft routing, self-attention vs cross-attention.

title: Multi-Head Attention
description: Running h attention heads in parallel, projecting into different subspaces, concatenating and projecting. Why multiple heads help.

title: Positional Encoding
description: Sinusoidal encodings, learned embeddings, RoPE (rotary position embeddings), ALiBi — why attention is permutation-equivariant without them.

title: Full Transformer Architecture
description: Encoder/decoder stacks, feedforward layers, layer norm placement (pre-norm vs post-norm), residual connections, the complete picture.

title: BERT & Encoder Models
description: Masked language modeling pretraining, bidirectional context, fine-tuning paradigm, NSP task, BERT variants (RoBERTa, DeBERTa).

title: GPT & Decoder Models
description: Autoregressive language modeling, causal masking, in-context learning, prompting, decoder-only architecture, KV cache mechanics.

title: Scaling Laws
description: Chinchilla scaling laws, compute-optimal training, power-law relationships between loss, parameters, data, and compute.

title: Fine-tuning (Full/LoRA/QLoRA)
description: Full fine-tuning vs parameter-efficient methods. LoRA: low-rank adapter matrices, rank selection. QLoRA: quantized base + LoRA. When to use each.

title: RLHF / DPO / PPO / GRPO
description: Human preference alignment. RLHF pipeline (reward model + PPO). Direct Preference Optimization as closed-form alternative. GRPO for group relative policy optimization.

title: Mechanistic Interpretability
description: Circuits, superposition, monosemanticity, activation patching, attention head analysis. Understanding what transformers compute internally.

title: Multimodal Models (CLIP/LLaVA)
description: Contrastive image-text pretraining (CLIP), vision encoders, visual instruction tuning (LLaVA), cross-modal attention architectures.

title: Efficient Inference (Quantization/Speculative Decoding)
description: INT8/INT4/FP8 quantization (GPTQ, AWQ), speculative decoding with draft models, flash attention, continuous batching, paged attention (vLLM).

title: ML Pipeline Architecture
description: Data ingestion, feature engineering, training, evaluation, serving — the full lifecycle. Orchestration with Airflow/Kubeflow, reproducibility.

title: Feature Stores
description: Online vs offline stores, point-in-time correctness, feature reuse, training-serving skew prevention. Tecton, Feast, Hopsworks.

title: Model Registry & Versioning
description: Model lineage, artifact tracking, A/B test assignments, canary deployments, rollback strategies. MLflow, Weights & Biases.

title: REST vs gRPC for ML Serving
description: HTTP/1.1 JSON vs HTTP/2 Protobuf. Latency tradeoffs, streaming, bidirectional communication. When to use each for model serving.

title: Batching Strategies
description: Static batching, dynamic batching, continuous batching for LLMs. Throughput vs latency tradeoff. GPU utilization optimization.

title: Latency vs Throughput
description: P99 latency, SLOs, Little's Law, queueing theory. Techniques: model distillation, caching, async serving, hardware selection.

title: KV Cache
description: Key-value caching for autoregressive generation. Memory footprint vs recomputation tradeoff. Multi-query attention, grouped query attention as KV cache compression.

title: Distributed Training (FSDP/DDP)
description: Data parallelism (DDP), tensor parallelism, pipeline parallelism, FSDP for sharding model states. ZeRO stages, gradient communication.

title: Vector Databases & HNSW
description: ANN search, HNSW graph construction (hierarchical navigable small world), IVF-PQ, Faiss. Recall vs latency tradeoffs.

title: RAG Pipelines
description: Retrieval-augmented generation: chunking strategies, embedding models, hybrid search, reranking, context window management, hallucination reduction.

title: Eval Frameworks
description: Offline evals (MMLU, HumanEval), LLM-as-judge, human preference data, A/B testing LLMs in production, regression detection.

title: Tool Use & Function Calling
description: Structured output for tool calls, JSON schema enforcement, parallel tool calls, tool selection reasoning, ReAct pattern.

title: Memory Architecture (Episodic/Semantic/Procedural)
description: In-context vs external memory. Episodic (conversation history), semantic (knowledge base), procedural (skill caching). Memory retrieval and management.

title: Multi-Agent Orchestration
description: Agent graphs, supervisor patterns, worker specialization, shared state management, async coordination, failure modes and recovery.

title: MCP Protocol
description: Model Context Protocol: standardized tool/resource/prompt interfaces for LLM applications. Server/client architecture, transport layers.

title: Time Series Analysis
description: AR/MA/ARIMA models, ACF/PACF interpretation, decomposition (trend/seasonality/residual), forecasting, ADF test for stationarity.

title: Stationarity & Autocorrelation
description: Weak vs strong stationarity, unit root tests (ADF, KPSS), cointegration, spurious regression, differencing to achieve stationarity.

title: Probability for Finance
description: Risk-neutral measure, expected value, variance, covariance, copulas, fat tails, kurtosis, VaR intuition, central limit theorem applications.

title: Factor Models (Fama-French)
description: CAPM, Fama-French 3/5-factor models, alpha decomposition, factor exposures, factor risk premia, multi-factor regression.

title: Portfolio Optimization (MVO/Risk Parity)
description: Mean-variance optimization, efficient frontier, Sharpe ratio maximization, risk parity, Black-Litterman, shrinkage estimators for covariance.

title: Risk Metrics (VaR/CVaR/Sharpe)
description: Value at Risk (historical, parametric, Monte Carlo), CVaR/Expected Shortfall, Sharpe/Sortino/Calmar ratios, max drawdown, stress testing.

title: Market Microstructure
description: Bid-ask spread, order book dynamics, market impact, adverse selection, Amihud illiquidity, price discovery, LOB models.

title: Alpha Generation & Decay
description: Alpha as excess return vs benchmark, signal construction, alpha decay half-life, turnover vs capacity tradeoff, orthogonalization of signals.

title: Backtesting (Correct Methodology)
description: Lookahead bias, survivorship bias, multiple testing problem, walk-forward validation, combinatorial purged cross-validation (CPCV), deflated Sharpe ratio.

title: IC/IR Framework
description: Information Coefficient (correlation of forecasts with outcomes), Information Ratio, Fundamental Law of Active Management: IR = IC × sqrt(breadth).

title: Regime Detection
description: Hidden Markov Models for market regimes, changepoint detection, clustering-based regime identification, regime-conditional strategies.

title: Derivatives & Options Pricing
description: Black-Scholes derivation, Greeks (delta/gamma/vega/theta), risk-neutral pricing, binomial trees, implied volatility surface.

title: Kelly Criterion & Position Sizing
description: Full Kelly derivation, fractional Kelly for robustness, relationship to log utility, multi-asset Kelly, practical position sizing considerations.

title: Execution Algorithms (TWAP/VWAP)
description: TWAP vs VWAP vs implementation shortfall. Market impact models, optimal execution (Almgren-Chriss), dark pools, smart order routing.

title: Genomics Basics (DNA/RNA/NGS)
description: Central dogma, DNA sequencing (Illumina/PacBio), NGS pipeline (FASTQ→alignment→variant calling), reference genome, variant types (SNP, indel, CNV).

title: Clinical Data (EHR/FHIR/ICD)
description: Electronic health records structure, FHIR standard, ICD-10 coding, SNOMED-CT, structured vs unstructured clinical data, de-identification.

title: Drug Discovery Pipeline
description: Target identification, hit-to-lead, lead optimization, preclinical, Phase I/II/III trials. ML applications at each stage. Attrition rates and costs.

title: AlphaFold Architecture
description: Multiple sequence alignments, Evoformer transformer blocks, structure module with IPA attention, end-to-end protein structure prediction.

title: Single Cell RNA-seq
description: scRNA-seq workflow, UMI counts, dimensionality reduction (UMAP/t-SNE), clustering, cell type annotation, trajectory analysis, doublet detection.

title: Sequence Modeling for Biology
description: DNA/protein language models (ESM, Evo), tokenization strategies, masked language modeling on sequences, downstream task fine-tuning.

title: Clinical NLP & Named Entity Recognition
description: Medical NER (diagnoses, medications, procedures), negation detection, temporal relations, UMLS normalization, clinical BERT models (BioBERT, ClinicalBERT).

title: Survival Analysis & Time-to-Event
description: Kaplan-Meier curves, log-rank test, Cox proportional hazards model, accelerated failure time models, censoring, competing risks.

title: Real World Evidence & Confounding
description: Observational study design, propensity score matching, instrumental variables in health, positive/negative controls, immortal time bias.

title: FDA SaMD Regulatory Pathway
description: Software as a Medical Device classification (Class I/II/III), 510(k) vs PMA, AI/ML-based SaMD guidance, predetermined change control plans.

title: Foundation Models for Biology
description: Multi-modal bio foundation models, genomic + transcriptomic + proteomic pretraining, zero-shot drug property prediction, virtual screening.

title: Multimodal Health AI
description: Fusing EHR, imaging (radiology/pathology), genomics, wearables. Cross-modal contrastive learning, missing modality handling, temporal alignment.

title: PSP / Patient Support Platform Architecture
description: Patient support program systems: adherence tracking, drug co-pay assistance, HIPAA-compliant data flows, prior authorization automation.
